author: śrīla premānanda⋅dāsa ṭhākura
verses:
ore mana! śuna śuna tu ati barbara
śata⋅sandhi⋅jarajara, peye ei kalevara,
kibā garva karicha antara

trayātmikā vyādhi jata, beḍiyā āchaye kata,
ki jāni kakhana kebā nāśe
‘e āmi āmāra’ bali, nija⋅prabhu pāsarili,
śamana-kiṅkara dekhi’ hāse

je deha āpana⋅jñāne, jatna kara’ rātri-dine,
vasana bhūṣaṇa kata veśa
paramātmā bhagavān, jabe habe antardhāna,
bhasma kīṭa kṛmi avaśeṣa

nidrāte paḍile mana, kothā ghara⋅dvāra⋅dhana,
strī⋅putra⋅bāndhava thāke kathi
ihāte nā lāge dhanda, tabu kārya kara manda,
nā cintile āpanāra gati

niti niti jīya mara, ite nā vicāra kara,
emati jāibe ekabāra
kahe dīna premānanda, bhaja kṛṣṇa⋅padadvandva,
māyā⋅pāśa ghucibe galāra
en_translation:
O my mind! Listen, you are very ignorant. Having attained this body which is subject to so many weaknesses, what pride do you harbor within?

Three-fold miseries surround you—who knows when one will destroy you? Saying "this is I" and "this is mine," you have forgotten your own Lord. The servants of death only laugh at your condition.

This body, which you consider your own and care for day and night with clothes and ornaments—when the Supreme Lord disappears from it, only ashes, worms, and insects will remain.

When the mind falls asleep, where then are your house, wealth, family, and friends? You are not confused by this reality, yet you continue to do evil deeds without thinking of your ultimate destination.

Daily you experience a kind of life and death, yet you do not consider that you will surely depart one day. The lowly Premānanda says: "Worship the twin lotus feet of Lord Kṛṣṇa, and the noose of illusion will be removed from your neck."
